content.observe: Apps and Framework on top, then Libraries and Android Runtime, then the Linux Kernel underneath everything — a different shape than iOS, same underlying problem.
content.fault: A library call went straight to the Linux kernel, bypassing the Android Runtime entirely. This stack expects that mediation, and it's missing.

hotspots.label: Applications & Framework
hotspots.detail: User-facing apps and the Java API framework they're built on.

hotspots.label: Native Libraries
hotspots.detail: C/C++ libraries providing core functionality like graphics and databases.

hotspots.label: Android Runtime
hotspots.detail: Executes app bytecode, one runtime instance per app.

hotspots.label: Linux Kernel
hotspots.detail: Manages hardware, memory, and process scheduling at the lowest layer.
